sql_array: CREATE TABLE regions (id UInt32, name String, parent_id UInt32, metadata String) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO regions VALUES (1, 'North', NULL, 'Region North'), (2, 'South', NULL, 'Region South'), (3, 'East', 1, 'Region East'), (4, 'West', 2, 'Region West');

SELECT regionToName(n1.id::UInt32, 'en') || (regionIn(n1.id::UInt32, n2.id::UInt32) ? ' is in ' : ' is not in ') || regionToName(n2.id::UInt32, 'en') AS relationship FROM regions AS n1 CROSS JOIN regions AS n2 WHERE n1.id IS NOT NULL AND n2.id IS NOT NULL;
SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN regionIn(n1.id::UInt32, n2.id::UInt32) = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS total_related FROM regions AS n1, regions AS n2 HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT id, name, regionIn(id, parent_id) AS is_parent FROM regions ORDER BY id HAVING is_parent = 0;
SELECT DISTINCT regionToName(n1.id::UInt32, 'en') || (regionIn(n1.id::UInt32, 3) ? ' matches with Region 3' : ' does not match') AS match_result FROM regions AS n1 WHERE name LIKE '%Region%';
CREATE TABLE extended_regions AS SELECT id, name, parent_id, metadata, COUNT(*) over(PARTITION BY parent_id) AS children_count FROM regions;
INSERT INTO extended_regions SELECT id, name, parent_id, metadata FROM regions;
SELECT COALESCE(NULLIF(regionToName(id, 'en'), ''), 'Unknown Region') AS region_name FROM extended_regions WHERE regionIn(id, 2) = 1;
SELECT a.id AS first_region, b.id AS second_region FROM regions AS a JOIN regions AS b ON regionIn(a.id::UInt32, b.id::UInt32) = 1 WHERE a.id != b.id;
